diagnostics.message: divisor not provably nonzero
notes:
right operand 'b' has type value (range -100 .. 100),
which includes zero.

no preceding conditional or subtype constraint establishes
b /= 0 on all paths reaching this division.
rule: D27 Rule 3 (Division by Provably Nonzero Divisor)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 133:
"The right operand of the operators /, mod, and rem shall be
provably nonzero at compile time."

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 134:
"If none of the conditions in paragraph 133 holds, the program
is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
the expression with a diagnostic."
suggestions:
or use a positive subtype that excludes zero:
type Positive_Value is range 1 .. 100;

diagnostics.message: dereference of possibly null access value
notes: P is of type value_ptr (access value), which does not exclude null.

no null check precedes this dereference on all paths reaching
this program point.
rule: D27 Rule 4 (Not-Null Dereference)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.4 paragraph 136:
"Dereference of an access value shall require the access subtype
to be not null. A conforming implementation shall reject any
dereference where the access subtype at the point of dereference
does not exclude null."

neg_own_double_move.safe:22:7: error: use of moved value 'Source'
  |
  | 22 |       Target2 = Source;  -- ERROR: Source has already been moved
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: the source was already moved earlier on this path.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.2 (move semantics)

neg_own_borrow_conflict.safe:25:11: error: lender 'Owner' is frozen by an active mutable borrow
  |
  | 25 |       v = Owner.value;  -- ERROR: lender accessed during active borrow
  |    |           ^~~~~
  |
  note: reads, writes, and moves of the lender are forbidden while the borrow is active.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.3 (mutable borrow freezes lender)

neg_own_use_after_move.safe:25:11: error: dereference of moved access value
  |
  | 25 |       v = Source.count;  -- ERROR: dereference of moved variable
  |    |           ^~~~~~
  |
  note: the access value was moved before this dereference.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 4 (Not-Null Dereference)

neg_own_lifetime.safe:19:14: error: returned borrow value cannot outlive lender 'Owner'
  |
  | 19 |       return Ref;  -- ERROR: borrowed value outlives local owner
  |    |              ^~~
  |
  note: borrowed and observed values must remain within the lender lifetime.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.3 / §2.3.4a (lifetime containment)

neg_own_observe_mutate.safe:22:7: error: observer 'Observer' provides read-only access
  |
  | 22 |       Observer.rate = 200;  -- ERROR: mutation through observer
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~
  |
  note: writes and moves through an active observer are not permitted.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.4 (observe is read-only; source frozen for write/move)

neg_own_target_not_null.safe:18:7: error: move target 'Target' is not provably null
  |
  | 18 |       Target = Source;  -- ERROR: target is not provably null
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: static analysis determined state 'NonNull' for the move target.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.2 (target of move must be provably null)

neg_own_source_maybe_null.safe:15:7: error: move source 'Source' is not provably non-null
  |
  | 15 |       Target = Source;  -- ERROR: source is not provably non-null
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: static analysis determined state 'Null' at this move site.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.2 (source of move must be provably non-null)

neg_own_anon_reassign.safe:19:7: error: anonymous access 'Ref' may only be assigned at its declaration
  |
  | 19 |       Ref = Other;  -- ERROR: anonymous access reassignment after declaration
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: borrow and observe locals are initialization-only.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.3 / §2.3.4a (anonymous access is initialization-only)

neg_own_anon_reassign_join.safe:24:7: error: anonymous access 'Ref' may only be assigned at its declaration
  |
  | 24 |       Ref = Other;  -- ERROR: merge must not reset init-only tracking
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: borrow and observe locals are initialization-only.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.3 / §2.3.4a (anonymous access is initialization-only)

neg_own_observe_requires_access.safe:16:7: error: observer 'Observer' must be initialized from X.Access
  |
  | 16 |       Observer : access constant config = Owner;  -- ERROR: must use Owner.Access
  |    |       ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: local observe uses anonymous access constant and requires an explicit .Access attribute.
  note: rule: Safe §2.3.4 (local observe must use .Access)

neg_rule1_overflow.safe:19:14: error: intermediate overflow in integer expression
  |
  | 19 |       return a * b;  -- ERROR: intermediate may exceed 64-bit range
  |    |              ^~~~~
  |
  note: static range analysis determines that the subexpression (a * b)
        has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
        which exceeds the 64-bit signed range
        [-9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807].
  note: a has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: b has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
        "If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
        range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
        arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the
        expression shall be rejected with a diagnostic."

neg_rule2_oob.safe:15:14: error: index expression not provably within array bounds
  |
  | 15 |       return arr (slot (idx));  -- ERROR: Idx may not be in 0..7
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: array 'arr' has index range slot (0 .. 7).
  note: index expression 'slot (idx)' has type integer, with range
        [integer'First .. integer'Last].
  note: static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
        [0 .. 7] on all execution paths.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:
        "The index expression in an indexed_component shall be provably
        within the array object's index bounds at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 132:
        "If neither condition holds, the program is nonconforming and
        the implementation shall reject it with a diagnostic identifying
        the indexed_component and the unresolvable bound relationship."
  suggestion: add a bounds check before indexing:
        if idx >= 0 and then idx <= 7 then
           return arr (slot (idx));
        end if;

neg_rule3_zero_div.safe:14:16: error: divisor not provably nonzero
  |
  | 14 |       return a / b;  -- ERROR: B may be zero
  |    |                  ^
  |
  note: right operand 'b' has type value (range -100 .. 100),
        which includes zero.
  note: no preceding conditional or subtype constraint establishes
        b /= 0 on all paths reaching this division.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 3 (Division by Provably Nonzero Divisor)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 133:
        "The right operand of the operators /, mod, and rem shall be
        provably nonzero at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 134:
        "If none of the conditions in paragraph 133 holds, the program
        is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
        the expression with a diagnostic."
  suggestion: add a guard before the division:
        if b /= 0 then
           return a / b;
        else
           return 0;  -- or handle the zero case
        end if;
  suggestion: or use a positive subtype that excludes zero:
        type Positive_Value is range 1 .. 100;

neg_rule4_null_deref.safe:14:14: error: dereference of possibly null access value
  |
  | 14 |       return P;  -- ERROR: P may be null
  |    |              ^
  |
  note: P is of type value_ptr (access value), which does not exclude null.
  note: no null check precedes this dereference on all paths reaching
        this program point.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 4 (Not-Null Dereference)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.4 paragraph 136:
        "Dereference of an access value shall require the access subtype
        to be not null. A conforming implementation shall reject any
        dereference where the access subtype at the point of dereference
        does not exclude null."
  suggestion: use a "not null access" subtype, or add an explicit null check:
        if P /= null then
           return P.all;
        end if;

neg_rule5_nan.safe:20:14: error: floating expression may be NaN at narrowing
  |
  | 20 |       return temp (x / y);  -- ERROR: 0.0/0.0 = NaN, cannot narrow
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~
  |

neg_result_unguarded.safe:36:14: error: access to variant field 'value' requires established discriminant 'ok'
  |
  | 36 |       return r.value;  -- REJECTED: R.OK not established
  |    |              ^~~~~~~
  |
  note: the discriminant 'ok' was not established on all paths before this access.

neg_result_mutated.safe:41:17: error: access to variant field 'value' requires established discriminant 'ok'
  |
  | 41 |          return r.value;          -- REJECTED: R.OK no longer established
  |    |                 ^~~~~~~
  |
  note: the discriminant fact for 'r' was invalidated by assignment or an out/in out call.

neg_rule5_div_zero.safe:19:46: error: floating divisor is not provably nonzero
  |
  | 19 |       return measurement (long_float (value) / long_float (scale));
  |    |                                                ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: division by a value that may be 0.0 can produce infinity or NaN.

neg_rule5_infinity.safe:19:14: error: floating expression may be infinite at narrowing
  |
  | 19 |       return bounded_float (big / small);  -- ERROR: result may be infinity
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |

neg_rule5_overflow.safe:19:14: error: floating expression is not provably within target range
  |
  | 19 |       return small_float (long_float (a) * long_float (b));
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |

neg_rule5_uninitialized.safe:19:14: error: floating expression is not provably initialized at narrowing
  |
  | 19 |       return bounded (x);  -- ERROR: X is uninitialized, may be NaN/infinity
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~
  |

neg_while_variant_not_derivable.safe:16:13: error: current proof surface cannot derive a supported Loop_Variant from this while condition
  |
  | 16 |       while value < goal
  |    |             ^~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: supported while-loop proof shapes are structural `Cursor != null` traversal and simple integer-bound `Lo < Hi` / `Lo <= Hi` conditions.
  note: rewrite the loop to match one of those forms, or use a different construct whose termination proof does not depend on an emitted Loop_Variant.

provably nonzero at compile time."

is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
the expression with a diagnostic."
suggestions:

diagnostics.message: intermediate overflow in integer expression
notes:
static range analysis determines that the subexpression (a * b)
has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
which exceeds the 64-bit signed range
[-9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807].
rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
"If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the

diagnostics.message: intermediate overflow in integer expression
notes:
static range analysis determines that the subexpression (a * b)
has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
which exceeds the 64-bit signed range
[-9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807].
rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
"If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the
expression shall be rejected with a diagnostic."

diagnostics.message: index expression not provably within array bounds
notes: array 'arr' has index range idx (1 .. 10).

index expression 'idx (a + b)' has type integer, with range
[integer'First .. integer'Last].

static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
[1 .. 10] on all execution paths.
rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:
"The index expression in an indexed_component shall be provably
within the array object's index bounds at compile time."

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 132:
"If neither condition holds, the program is nonconforming and
the implementation shall reject it with a diagnostic identifying
the indexed_component and the unresolvable bound relationship."

[integer'First .. integer'Last].","static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
[1 .. 10] on all execution paths.","rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)","per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:

diagnostics.message: dereference of possibly null access value
notes: P is of type value_ptr (access value), which does not exclude null.

no null check precedes this dereference on all paths reaching
this program point.
rule: D27 Rule 4 (Not-Null Dereference)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.4 paragraph 136:
"Dereference of an access value shall require the access subtype
to be not null. A conforming implementation shall reject any
dereference where the access subtype at the point of dereference
does not exclude null."
